factors.name: Tech selloff spillover
factors.summary: Massive tech rout on Friday (mentioned as interrupting two-month rally) is dragging broader indices; Dow is heavyweight on tech via MSFT and other components. Momentum negative entering the week.

factors.name: Geopolitical escalation (Iran-Israel)
factors.summary: Iran fired missiles at Israel on Sunday; fragile ceasefire since April is in jeopardy. Oil spiked >$2 per barrel. While Trump is attempting to de-escalate and Netanyahu agreed to wait, uncertainty premium remains elevated and threatens risk sentiment broadly.

factors.name: Oil price surge
factors.summary: Oil jumped sharply on Iran strikes, raising input-cost concerns for Dow industrials (transportation, manufacturing). Airlines cutting capacity (Air New Zealand, LATAM citing fuel shock); margin compression risk for cyclical names.

factors.name: Strong US jobs data + rate expectations
factors.summary: Bond traders expect largest CPI surge in years this week (Bloomberg); strong jobs report fueled expectations for Fed rate hike. Higher-for-longer rates hurt dividend-heavy Dow (UNH, KO, PG, etc.) and multiple compression in rate-sensitive financials.

factors.name: Japan GDP beat + yen strength signals
factors.summary: Japan Q1 GDP revised to 1.8% q/q annualized vs 1.3% expected; current account surplus beat. USD/JPY above 160 with BOJ intervention risk, but domestic strength is not a direct Dow catalyst.

factors.name: Sector rotation away from tech
factors.summary: Investors fleeing tech into health insurers, banks, retailers (MarketWatch); Dow is still heavily weighted to tech (MSFT, AAPL exposure via funds), so rotation headwind in near term.

factors.name: China outbound investment crackdown + BoP constraints
factors.summary: China announced 'illegal' outbound investment crackdown; combined with Middle East tensions blocking Strait of Hormuz (10–13M bpd shut in per Investinglive), global supply chain risk rising. CAT, BA exposure to China demand at risk.

factors.name: Dollar strength (risk-off bid)
factors.summary: AUD/USD under pressure (0.7024 early Monday); broader USD strength on geopolitical safe-haven bid. Dow has ~50% international revenue; stronger USD = translation headwind for multinational earnings.

factors.name: Credit/IG spread risk
factors.summary: No explicit spreads mentioned, but combination of higher rates, oil shock, and geopolitical uncertainty typically widens credit. Dow financials (GS, JPM) sensitive to spreads; corporate borrowing pressure rising (Japanese corporates ramping M&A, leverage).

factors.name: Industrial input costs (metals, freight)
factors.summary: Oil surge + geopolitical uncertainty raising transportation/logistics costs. Cass Freight, rail, port data not yet reported this week, but trajectory is cost-push inflationary for Dow industrials.

key_levels.resistance: 51,000–51,100 (prior swing high before Friday tech rout)

screening.narrative: The Dow is down 1.02% in 24h, caught in a dual downdraft from Friday's tech selloff and Sunday's Iran-Israel escalation. Oil spiked >$2 on geopolitical risk, raising input-cost concerns for industrials and transportation; simultaneously, bond traders are pricing in a CPI surprise this week and potential Fed rate hike, which hammers dividend payers and rate-sensitive financials that anchor the index. USD strength on safe-haven demand and ~50% international revenue exposure to Dow components create additional translation headwinds. Trump's attempt to contain Iran tensions and Netanyahu's agreement to delay retaliation provide some relief, but the ceasefire remains fragile and market is pricing elevated risk premium into the open Monday.
catalysts_ahead: US CPI (expected this week, bond traders pricing surprise surge) — major rate/equity impact
Apple WWDC 2026 (Monday) — tech sentiment barometer post-selloff
ISM Manufacturing PMI, ISM Services PMI — direct read on Dow industrial composition
Fed Funds futures market (reacting to rate-hike odds) — 10Y yield moves
Dow component earnings updates (UNH, GS, BA, CAT forward guidance) — cyclical momentum check
Further Iran-Israel developments or ceasefire breakdown — geopolitical risk reset
Oil (WTI/Brent) holding >$90 threshold — continued input-cost pressure signal
China PMI, trade data — demand shock risk for Dow heavyweights (CAT, BA)
screening.risk_warning: If Trump successfully negotiates Iran deal and ceasefire holds (reducing oil price spike), tech stabilizes, and CPI surprise is benign (no Fed hike needed), Dow could reverse sharply and re-test highs. Conversely, if Iran conflict escalates further, ceasefire breaks, or CPI shock triggers aggressive Fed tightening, downside could accelerate to 50,000–49,500.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is already underway (down 1.02% in 24h, Phase 2); avoid chasing shorts here. Wait for retest of 50,900–51,000 resistance or a brief relief rally on ceasefire headlines, then re-short on momentum failure. Alternatively, wait for CPI print mid-week to see if rate-hike expectations stabilize before entering new positions.
